rows.text:
I hoped Id never have to write a bad review, but here it is. This coffee maker sucks dick. Ill start with the pot. Its a bullshit pot, because its got this stupid plastic lid which pours fine at first but blocks the last half cup of water! To get all the water out you have to flip the pot completely upside down so the opening points straight downwards, at which point the remaining half cup of water just goes everywhere. Like what!? Why!? Surely its not that hard to make a pot that can pour all its contents right?! It doesnt help that the hole for the water is hard to reach and really small. Id love to see a video of somebody filling the water on this thing properly, cause i dont believe its possible.
Did none of the engineers test this thing before approving it? Its seriously ridiculous how bad it is.
Heres why I'm ashamed. I read all these reviews and brushed them off thinking "these guys are fucking morons they just dont know how to pour a coffee pot". No, I was wrong, or I am also a moron like them. This thing is seriously stupid.
The rest of the feature work fine, sure. But no Mr. Coffee, you still get only 1 star because every single morning Im reminded that Im the idiot that bought a coffee maker where i cant even pour all the coffee out of the pot, and cant fill the fricking thing without making a mess. I dont care that everything else works, the o
rows.title: WTF how could you mess up a coffee maker this bad!?

rows.text:
A few points to further enlighten the above reviewer with the self-proclaimed world class palate, self-styled biting wit, and self-affirmed firm deathgrip on logic and reason:

1. 'Truffle Essence' is not an actual infusion of real truffles of any sort into anything. Almost no 'truffle' products contain any real truffles, fresh or otherwise. No dried ones, no ground ones, no back-alley knockoffs, no mushrooms that maybe went to high school with a real truffle and sat at the same lunch table because they were 'cool with each other'. Nothing. This product is no exception. There are no truffles in this. At all. Your tongue is a liar.

It does, however, contain synthetically made organosulfurs which are produced both for industrial reasons and to mimic real truffles for rubes wanting to feel fancy. Doping a bottle of slick, oily, cloying pseudo-sriracha with lab-produced pretend truffle farts is the functional equivalent of pissing in a bottle of Sweet Baby Ray's while saying a prayer and calling it Hot Jesus Juice. It's still just hot piss in a bottle.

2. It does not taste 'exactly like black truffles'. Or any truffles. At all.

It does taste exactly like formaldehyde and kerosene swirled around with a bit of gas station kimchi that's been warming down the front of a hobo's pants. How do I know? To give this comment validity, I
rows.title: Like drinking literal garbage.

rows.text:
The below review I posted months ago.

The game has been updated a couple times since then. They added new weapon variants, some new characters, and weapons.

To sum it up. This game is still terrible.

Because it's such a terribly buggy game that freezes often in multiplayer especially against a specific enemy type called the kett and when one enemy called the fiend is in play. This happens so often most players know about this issue. An issue that I can guarantee will never be fixed. As I said, the buggy nature of this game resulted in my being unable to load the game. I only put about 50+ hours into the boring and terrible single-player, but I figured I may still force myself to play the terrible multiplayer. So I deleted and redownloaded the game. Now I reserved this game (big mistake) with some of the bonus material. After redownloading the game, this "bonus" material was no longer available. As a result, all my previous saves were nullified and would not load. So, this meant I had to start the single player again. I got about 15 hours in until I couldn't take it anymore. The cheesy lines, the bad graphics, the terrible gameplay, the boring exploration, the clumsy six-wheeled car/tank thing.

But I kept playing the multiplayer (MP) and it is just awful. From basic game mechanics to gameplay to reliability to playability, the
rows.title: A Review for the Lover of ME3's Multiplayer

rows.text:
For fans of the older Halo games, and even the newer ones such as Halo: Reach, expecting something better or at least up to par than the previous Halo games, this game is not for you. Halo 4 consists of a poorly pieced together campaign with less "Halo goodness" and more of Cortana getting psycho bitchy at everyone, UNSC soldiers calling scientists "Eggheads", and Master Chief getting thrown around. The story line is a solid meh, and the easter eggs feel forced and unoriginal (for example they more often than not reference an easter egg from an older Halo game, or a RvB quote or something). Rather than Firefight, Halo 4 has a mode called "Spartan Ops", a 50-episode minigame where your multiplayer Spartan must repeat the same 5 missions over again 10 times in the same maps from the campaign, with slightly varying outcomes each episode (for example one episode, you might need to save someone on foot, and in another one you need to save someone in a warthog). A very pissy spartan commander chick yells at you through each episode.
The multiplayer has a few good changes, but mostly it feels dry and buggy. My internet connection isn't great, but I've never experienced more lag-based problems in one match than I have with Halo 4. Not sure why that is. Sometimes the countdown to the game starting will freeze and your screen will be blurred over and you can hardly see your gun for
rows.title: Sad excuse of a Halo game

rows.text:
When I saw that there was a male-version of 'Skinny Bitch' coming out, I was excited. I read 'Skinny Bitch' and, despite much of the criticism, found it to be one of those life-changing books. It convinced me that going vegetarian was the way to go and that trying to be vegan was also a worthwhile venture. By reading 'Skinny Bitch' and following some of the advice it contained, I managed to lose a good 40 or 50 pounds, with the help of exercise in addition to the nutrition tips.

Now, 'Skinny Bastard'. Here's my advice; if you're a guy and you were too embarrassed to be seen toting around and flipping through 'Skinny Bitch', this is your book. If you read 'Skinny Bitch', don't expect to get much more out of this book. Wisely, I read through some of the chapters and flipped through the book at a local bookstore and was astonished to find that someone had the audacity to pretty much take 'Skinny Bitch', pack in a few more pages, change the packaging, make it 'speak' more to men and call it a new book. This is a blatant rip-off of one book pretty much attempting to spawn a franchise.

In summary, good material, but read 'Skinny Bitch' or 'Skinny Bastard', not both. 'Skinny Bastard' is for men and looks like it may have a few additional tid-bits that its female equivalent doesn't. Otherwise, don't waste your time and money on a book you've probably already have
rows.title: 'Skinny Bastard' nothing more than a re-packaged 'Skinny Bitchy'

rows.text:
Na na na na naaaa!

*Disclaimer: This post contains the story of chicken harvest… by said knife. 1. I live on a farm. 2. We raise animals for food (like eggs and milk and pork). 3. Ain’t no one got time for aggressive animals.

-Bought a “nice” 8 month old rooster from the hatchery I got my Australorps from the year before. Because I thought we could hatch some chicks. I named the rooster Chanticleer, for obvious reasons. He stayed in quarantine for two weeks away from my flock. Took him out and for two to three weeks, he was the low man on the totem pole. The girls picked on him a little and put him in his place. He was friends with our male duck. He SEEMED sweet! After that time, he became a real aggressive douche!

It started off with him being mean to the younger flock of chickens. Then he would bully the female ducks and his ex-best bud, our male duck. He was super rough with the hens he was mounting - like one had scabs and was going bald. Suddenly, he was getting vent on the regular and this cock turned into a real dick!

As time went on, he started getting aggressive with the kids and with me. I changed his name to Earl after the Chicks (previously known as the Dixie Chicks) song and found myself singing about his upcoming death often. You see, beautiful Earl was filled only with spite. He had pure evil running through his heart a
rows.title: (Great knife!) Because Earl had to die!

rows.text:
As a heterosexual woman who adores men, but always gets to be "the friend" I had high hopes for this book. But the sea of hipster profanity left me wanting to run to a Bible Study. Honestly, how is saying Fuck-shit-cunt-pussy all the time "creative" let alone "funny?" The intro was the best part. I did get a few good laughs, such as how hard it is to get and maintain a tennis ball butt. The rest, not so much. Yes, it takes work to look like this, but Dolly Parton said that back in Steel Magnolias--its not news.

There was so much promise in exploring how a tom boy can grow up and still be a happily heterosexual woman, wife and mom. But it was all about "me" her potty mouth and, naturally, how fab NYC is. There's life outside of NYC people. Honestly.
rows.title: So much promise, so little delivered

rows.text: I was extremely skeptical of this … I mean let’s face it, it’s just a giant container of the stickiest substance on earth lmfao… but when I tell you this stuff works… IT WORKS..!!! Here’s the story!! …… So it all started when A pair of pigeons laid two eggs on my balcony I could very well of just threw them off my balcony and got rid of the problem from the beginning but I was trying to be a decent human being and decided to let them stay and raise the little chicks… Big mistake I should’ve been like the rest of society and been a shitty human being a.k.a. throwing the eggs off the balcony… had I known what was to come… Approximately 21 days later the eggs hatched give or take a few days I did a bunch of research online it said from start to finish it should take no longer than three months total time from the time of conception for the eggs to hatch grow up and fly away and leave the nest… this is the prime reason I do not believe everything I read on the Internet… Five months later I had a family of pigeons living on my balcony they had taken over shit everywhere all over everything and refused to leave no matter what I did they were not afraid of me due to the fact that I gave them water and birdseed when they were first born keep in mind this was 3 to 4 months ago those little bastards not forget those 2 to 3 days of birdseed and water and they decided to hold onto Hope tha
rows.title: SAVED MULTIPLE PIGEONS LIVES..

rows.text:
Advanced notice: This review will be long and as comprehensive and detailed as I can be with updates as time goes on. If you like short reviews it's 5 stars and skip to the bottom for a summary. If you like details about another perspective and experience read on...

****Update 08-08-2018***
I just got off the phone with Primary Arms tech guy and after asking questions the helpful gentleman looked up the information for me.

To paraphrase the phone call this scope will work with Mk262 from a 16-18" barrel zero'd at 200 yards or 2" high at 100 yards.
It will also work for M80 ball as well as up to 180 grain I forget the bullet and velocity now but iirc it was pretty standard.
Inside the manual for people who don't have an issue reading (bifocals mess with my eyes to much) it sounded like it should work with pretty much every common load for both calibers, but consult the manual yourself by downloading it before purchasing to verify.

So with this information in hand I am incredibly pleased so far and am looking forward to trying to get to the range this evening to revisit some things I mentioned in my original review. I now have my eye relief set as close as I'm able to on my 10/22 and if able to go will be taking a .223Wylde chamber as well!
Yay me!!!!

Also the more I handle this scope it seems like it may wel
rows.title: Initial impressions Awesome scope at an entry level price!!!

rows.text:
Let me tell you a story. I tried Monster iSport Immersion with control talk, Klipsch Image S4i, Sony XBA-4IP and Boss MIE2i.

Each of these midrange earphones, and Sony XBA-4IP as of high range are totally different in their sound quality, comfort, quality and feel in various ways.
I tried Monster iSport, which seals sound in a better way than rest of others, but sounds mix of everything emphasizing bass. Then I tried Klipsch Image S4i, which is comparatively a huge improvement on comfort and feel along with considerably admirable bass and effective vocal clarity.

But, it didn't satisfy me because I was in search of a better earphones that I can have all what I would expect from. Then I went to buy Boss and then Sony. All piece of shit. Nothing that is worth to glorify: hey, I've these earphones! You will lose every penny you spent on them and never be able to compensate with these all brands I mentioned unless one maker comes ahead and delivers what we really want.

In my totally personal experience:

1. Earphones suck because they hurt and annoy you instead of pleasing you for extended period of use. Must of us hate this because we want to enjoy music. And most of the brands just suck never being able to realize what comfort means to an extended time music listener. So we desperately want a kind of earphones that at least be abl
rows.title: All Earphones Suck

rows.text: The first book Diamonds is ridiculous and Delilah is naive to the point of idiocy. She is nice to a fairy and in turn is given the gift to make huge sums of money. This is handy, because of a crime her father committed (that is not revealed) Delilah and her family went from being super rich to broke and socially disgraced. However this is clearly the rich version of broke because Delilah still can afford to hang out with the same social set, and does not seem to have a job other than throwing charity fundraisers with her friends. One of these friends is Chas, a hot, rich, businessman. What is his business? No idea. He is a womanizer and serial Fiancé. Soon after discovering her windfall Delilah runs to tell her friend Chas so that he can help her invest, seeing as he's a "businessman" and all. Chas however is in dire financial straits. So upon learning of her new financial status instead of asking his friend for a loan, he whips out the returned engagement ring from his latest ex-Fiancé and asks her to marry him. But he's not a bad guy because his plan is not to actually marry her. He's just going to temporarily take over her finances, take her money and invest it in his company, then pay her back with interest. All without her knowledge of course. And even though they have not had even a remotely romantic relationship Delilah says yes to his proposal and the shenanigans begin.
rows.title: Suspense, of belief...

rows.text:
OMG!!! Where do I start? I should start out by saying I REALLY, REALLY HATE this book. I've read a couple of this author's novels over the years, they were okay, but this one takes the cake for being a vile, piece of garbage that should've been sent to the reject pile. Written around 1980, I guess the bodice rippers were making their way out of the disco era where it was common for women to be marginalized, seen as sexual objects, and used and abused in every romance novel by Rosemary Rogers and the like. I have a feeling Ms. Robards was trying to emulate the former. Anyone remember Sweet Savage Love? If you haven't. Then good for you. I literally burned my copy years ago it was so awful. But I digress.

This clinker has a thirty-something man going through what I guess you could call a middle age crisis. Somewhere along the line, Justin's misspent youth was snatched from him when he took on the guardianship of a young girl, Megan when she was five. I suppose his later abuse of her is rooted in deep hostility. Or it could be because he's a sociopath who preys on seventeen year-old girls fresh out of boarding school. That's where dear little Megan has spent most of her life. They hardly know each other. He pays the bills, provides room and board, and doesn't have to deal with her at all until she comes home ready and ripe for the plucking. This entire story is horrific
rows.title: Run, run as fast you can...from this shocking disgrace of a book!

rows.text:
Dark Souls, as you've probably read from the other 323 reviews here, is a dark fantasy RPG legendary for its difficulty, obtuse-ness and mostly excellent combat system. When tasked to explain this game to one of my buddies recently, this is exactly what I said: "It's an awesome game, but you're going to die about 500 times at least. But you should still give it a try!" Not surprisingly, he hasn't tried it yet haha.

When I first started playing this game, there was an immediate sense of bewilderment and awe (especially when you first hit Firelink Shrine - the hub area - with its unique violin music and etc). There was an armored guy sitting down near a bonfire. I tried talking to him but he seemed at the time to mostly ramble on about strange things and laugh at me. (Actually, a TON of NPCs in this game like to laugh when you talk to them for some weird reason). He spoke of bells to ring, so me with my crap starting armor and heater shield walked down to what looked like the next area I should go to- a nice little cemetary just a staircase away from the bonfire.

Walking down those stairs, I was soon beset upon by some skeletons. WOW. All I can say is that unlike maybe every other RPG in existance, Dark Souls' skeletons are NOT the pushovers you'd expect. They move incredibly quickly, slicing and dicing you to bits causing heavy bleeding.. and then "YOU DIED
rows.title: Excellent but ultimately too hard for its own good

rows.text:
There are technically 5 games on here, and I'll go over them one at a time explaining why this is the best game ever and you should sell Modern Warfare 12 or whatever it is now and get this instead. So let's begin shall we?

Half Life 2/Portal: They're good.

Now onto the REAL star of the show here, the highly addicting TF2! The mentally challenged dislike this game on first impression basis because it looks too cartoony and they want another brown/grey grizzled average American Joe Hero to play as instead. Let them rot in their purgatory of mediocrity, Team Fortress 2 is a bright and unique looking game akin to a violent Pixar movie where 9 mercenaries with unique abilities kill each other for unimportant reasons. Aaaaaand the mercenaries are:

Scout: A hotheaded Boston with a baseball bat shotgun and pistol, players can use his speed to their advantage which is about all the Scout has as a use, since he can't take very much damage before dying. People who are really good with the Scout game use his nimbleness to ambush enemy teams by jumping in and terrorizing the enemy team while they frantically try to relatiate to the little Boston Bastard. On the flipside people who suck with the Scout are usually just run around like everyone else without using agility and get their asses kicked in a matter of seconds.

Soldier: A stereotypical Ame
rows.title: Warning: Extremely Addicting; Proceed with Caution

rows.text:
I eagerly awaited Mass Effect 2, preordered it, and counted down the days for 3 months. Overall, it's a good experience. I think most of the gripes I had with the game might be due to overthinking everything, but some changes really annoyed me. Minor spoilers ahead.

GRAPHICS: 8/10
The graphics got a major overhaul this time around. The lighting is superb. The faces during conversation look just as awful as ever, and it's difficult to spot expressions or emotions, just like in the first one. The camera during conversations is a lot better, though. It moves with you, shakes during explosions, an has some interesting effects that make the conversations seem a lot more than just two heads talking. The environments are a lot more varied, and by a lot more varied, I really mean a lot more. You can actually distinguish between planets, unlike the first one, in which planets differed only in ground color and number/placement of annoying mountains(exploration). Some armor textures are lacking, and there's a lot of clipping near the shoulderpads...still.

Of course, graphics don't really matter at all since Mass Effect shouldn't about the visual experience.

SOUND: 6/10
The original ME soundtrack with all its mesmerizing techno beats and synth is pretty much gone. You can still play them in your cabin, but only your cabin. I didn't fin
rows.title: Not what I was expecting

rows.text:
I'm very disappointed! I was looking forward reading this sequel to Phoenix Dark Messiah which was a fun post apocalyptic novel with Commando mixed themes in it. The start of the book Summer Song after the events of Dark Messiah leaving Trench aka The Phoenix with no reason only to stay in another town. Trench continues he quest to return to New York hoping his son & wife are still alive after WWIII broke out.

First page Trench enters a town to get food, ammo and gas however quickly learns the town's people capture people and steal whatever they own. Trench barely escapes and is saved by old man who takes him to Las Vegas which is owned by The Sheik. After learning a wanted bounty is on The Phoenix, Sheik makes Trench a deal to enter in his death sport or be sold off to his enemy. Trench enters in a Death Race like sport with muscle cars covered in armour and weapons. Trench wins the race taking out all the other drivers then makes a escape and almost getting killed again when a fighter jet shows up and fires a missile hitting his car and crashing into a tunnel yet somehow walking away unburned or uninjured.

And this is where things get messed up. Trench is told by a group of people mostly women called Earthwomb stating he is the choice one to fight the evil and to proof this he must have sex with Earthwomb leader and then the story starts jumping all over
rows.title: A Total Mess!!!

rows.text:
(SPOILER ALERT),.but does it really matter???
I hate the star system because as they say one man's trash is another man's.. well let's just be honest here...trash..
To be or not to be British is the question but what is the answer???? For the life of me, I can not understand how a movie that is obviously shot in the UK that also features British actors, would try to moonlight as being in the USA?
Their awful American accents are abysmal at best.
If the cast would try as hard to act as they do trying to sound American, then this film would be...nawww who am I kidding?, it would be like if you sprayed perfume on a pile of crap, not only would it still stink but it would still be crap.
Why then do some of the students and teachers still have their British accent? ( which at times reminds me of an Aussie accent, In fact, I thought that this was an Australian film but I read that it was British)
QUESTION: What rhymes with Cupid but begins with the letter S?
Figure out that riddle and you'll understand the rest of this review..
This holiday crapfest begins looking like the beginning of a really cheap looking Disney movie.
A semi-animated book tells the story of the mythological creature called Cupid. However, they twist and bend the true lore to their will to fit the story-line for this movie.
Here it is in a nutshell... A Valentines
rows.title: TO BE ..OR NOT TO BE.. BRITISH?

rows.text:
I thought this was well written, the story-line flowed, and I had an actual feel for both heroes (which isn't that common, let me tell you). The reason I found it a bit disappointing was some of the glaring old misogyny hangups. I don't need strong women in an M/M Romance. I understand that by its very nature, M/M romance is going to focus on male-male interaction. Okay. But when the ONLY female character is presented as a slut, given no realistic motivation for her actions, and is also TREATED like a slut and dismissed by the other characters, I have a problem. The entire Sarah thing felt tacked on, entirely unnecessary, and the way she behaved made no sense when we consider who she was presented to be in book one. It was as if she was turned into a interfering tramp just to get a half chapter scene of jealousy from Logan. Then he does the slut shaming by accusing her of trying to win Kyle "with a set of tits".
Yeah, no thanks.
If the only female characters you're capable of writing are the elderly mother (non threatening) and the interfering whore (artificially threatening), than please don't bother at all.
PS: the manage scene with the woman Logan picked up in the bar? Wow. Was it your intention to have the heroes come off as bastards, or was that just a side effect?
rows.title: Okay story, good writing. Same old dismissive crap with female characters though

rows.text:
This wasn't the worst thing I've read but it certainly wasn't the best either. The sex scenes are not very well written in my opinion. After the first 2 or 3 I started skimming them. Too much porn talk (are there people that really talk like that during sex???) I felt like I was reading a script for a porn and after the first scene they all just ran together and was the same thing over and over. Not hot at all.

I also am having a hard time liking the charcters. Talon is an asshole! Yeah he has a terrible past and has reasons for being aloof and defensive but he sure makes it hard to like him. And Jade doesnt know about his past and just lets him treat her like that. If a guy said all those things to me and then said "i want you to suck my cock" I'd tell the asshole to take a hike. I mean, what the fuck? If she already knew about everything in his past or at least had a pretty good idea it would be one thing, but she doesnt so it just makes her sound like a twit.

If I hadnt already purchased the second book when i purchased this one i wouldn't continue to read this series. I still might not.
rows.title: I wanted so bad to like this book.

rows.text: Madame de Pompadour just like the prick of a French King's (Louis XV) other concubines was nothing but a whore and who should not be celebrated for being a survivor but rather a prostitute! The Queen was not a bore but rather a lonely woman who deeply loved her husband despite the fact that he was a bastard to her and cheated on her repeatedly with low lives such as this whore! The only good thing that I can say about her is that she was cordial to the long suffering Queen Marie Leszczyñska when his other whores had snubbed her, and the Queen herself is said to have said of her "If there must be a mistress, better her than any other."
rows.title: Madame de Pompadour was a whore

rows.text:
In yet another blunder for the New 52, Geoff Johns (Remember when he did awesome stuff like JSA or Green Lantern?) and Jim Lee (Who designed every crappy new costume) hooked up Superman and Wonder Woman. Because they both have powers, and the staff of DC is composed of very immature 12 year olds. So lets drop and degrade Lois, so we can have them fly around and fight and kiss.

Following this revelation of pure shallowness, they decided to give Superman and Wonder Woman their own book. For the first time in decades- Wonder Woman has a second comic, and all they had to do was attach her to Superman to do it because Wonder Woman was last interesting in 1947. Hoping to capture the brain-dead teenage market, they decided to make this book try and appeal to the Twilight crowd. Team Lois or Team Wonder Woman? How about Team FireDidio?

Chales Soule trained as a lawyer, so his lack of a soul probably helped him pen this dreck. He is probably has too much on his plate and knows that this is not going to win any awards because it is blatantly a corporate created idea, so he dropped this hot mess after 12 issues. After a crossover orchestrated by Scott Lobdell. Let's take a look at the title for this volume: Power Couple. Like a Hollywood Power Couple, aren't they clever?

Superman is badly characterized here. He is a douche bag, like most New 52
rows.title: Super-Douche and Wonder Bitch

rows.text:
it just get too absurd and comical when the detective has a a bizarre sexual fetish of being dominated by a fat and hideous old women. They made the other female characters in the story much prettier than you would usually find in upstate New York I live in nyc but have a hunting lodge estate in upstate the women are not attractive at all. They could have at least made the dominatrix attractive like some of the others sexualized characters.
beyond that its interesting bc in the first few episodes you really hate this women bc she lies to her husband and family and says she was a whore and you just thunk she's the typical lower class promiscuous women who eventually finds a sucker to marry her and take care of her after deceiving him about everything. some things come to light through flashbacks and the detectives investigating that make her appear more sympathetic, but not much. She was still a slutty girl who behaved inn a trashy way but its avg teenage trashy stuff..the kind of lies most women who are not ladies have. mainly this interesting contrived conspiracy of child abuse with upper middle class rich people from new but established American families emerges. it could be interesting but that all falls apart and the plot twist at the end is kind of lame,.
I just had to fast forward all the parts with the fat old bitch who has weird sex with the cop bc she
rows.title: Interesting mystery for a little but

rows.text:
I figured, I should read more Irish authors and I figured, I should read more female Irish authors. So, I picked this book up, along with a few others, trying to fill a 4-for-3 quota. I read it all the way through.
(I adore short stories.)
These stories are utter rubbish.
Had they been written by men, the book and the authors would be excoriated for being misogynistic cretins obsessed with their own gender. As such, the book is filled with un-ironic slut-shaming, un-ironic figure-bashing, god-awful romance, some staying in an abusive relationship. Almost every single story is about or has a strong component of, why women need a man in their life.
Just one. And he’s the empowered one. He may screw around. She may not.

With that being written, here is a review of each story, in the order they appear:

Soulmates by Marian Keyes
The perfect couple slowly grows old together until finally their house of cards, or possibly house of wood, comes crashing down around them much to the chagrin of their neighbors/classmates in the insular world of the story.
“’So was it a disaster?’” Peter begged Tim. ‘Did they try to kill each other?’
Watched by seven avid pairs of eyes, Tim shook his head sorrowfully. ‘They got on like a house on fire. They’re going to do it again in July.’”
That should properly set the tenor.
Along with th
rows.title: Utter, dis-empowering rubbish

rows.text:
While most women/females go by doctors instructions as most GYN doctors will quickly tell you not to duche, use feminine hygiene sprays, powders etc as they are said to be the primary reason for YEAST and other Bacterial Infections. Said to be the result in sending the PH Balance levels off. And as a result I know lots of women that DONT DOUCHE at all. HOWEVER, I have become a fan of the douche for a few reasons despite Doctors orders. But before I quickly get into those few reasons, I want to confirm that while using OTC douche product solutions (already mixed solution in a package) in the past particularly the spring fresh, tropical medley, Autumn flowers, even medicated & baking soda (premixed) douche solutions, as well as the feminine hygiene sprays they have all caused disruption of my PH balance leading to Yeast infections and Bacterial infections as well (Just as my GYN has said that they would). However they did provide "temporary freshness", covering up/masking odors. But i stoped using them.

Thats when I started mixing vinegar (regular or apple cidar both work great)and water myself, into my reusable douche syringe bottle. I found that when properly mixed/diluted- it worked better than the store purchased pre mixed Douche. I found myself using this mixure whenever I felt like it without triggering any infections. Then I learned about HERBAL DOUCHE (a natura
rows.title: Needs to be in the feminine hygiene section of every drug store!

rows.text:
I suppose my review won't be that popular, I believe Ambercrombie can do no wrong for some.

First off, this was the audiobook. Not my favorite reader (he mimics Johnny Depp's Jack Sparrow and for other voices sounds like he's doing terrible Elizabethan Shakespeare characters), but he did well enough. Lots of rolled "R"s and he says ass like "Awwws."

In a nutshell, a formidable mercenary woman goes on an "adventure" to avenge her brother's death, her attempted murder, with a ragged band of ruffians, a group barely held together against urges to all kill each other.

I really struggle with spoilers when I just don't really like a book or a character. I didn't *hate* this book... I just... I can't deny it's gigantic failings and clumsy character development. And by "struggle with" i mean I don't care that I'm spoiling the book. I'm angry I finished it.

Our heroin is damaged - her near death left her body broken and scarred. Her fighting hand in ruin, her spirit broken and driven by anger. We are informed her beloved brother was stabbed in the neck, and both of them thrown off a parapet...but it takes the length of the book for us to slowly learn more about her brother, her quest to avenge him, and her true character. We have to hear about her broken body a whole lot, don't worry if you miss it the first few times. We see her seek
rows.title: Poorly written characters, especially the female lead, lazy plot devices, and no story arcs.  Why is this writer popular?

rows.text:
*DNF*

I've reached my limit with this book. It is too irritating for me to continue. The hero is an immature, selfish pig. He leaves for five years after being forced to marry his childhood friend and ignores any attempts from his father to get him to come home. He's angry that he was forced to marry Sileas--he's horrified at being stuck with such a scrawny, unattractive girl--and he blames her for getting them into that situation. In his mind he's not married since he didn't consummate the marriage. He doesn't care (doesn't even consider at all) how bad things might have been for Sileas after being abandoned by her husband. It's her fault after all...

For her part, Sileas is still pining for Ian. She has a great guy hanging around her who loves her and wants to marry her. He tries to convince her to ask for an annulment or get a divorce because he wants to be with her and give her everything Ian hasn't. He's convinced that five years is long enough to wait. Too bad he's not the hero! But Sileas is still waiting for Ian to show up and fulfill her dreams of really being loved by him.

Ian comes back and expects to be God's gift to his family for returning. He's still pissed that he has to deal with the wife situation, though. He just wants to end it and be done with it. At least until he sees Sileas. Suddenly all he can think about is how hard she
rows.title: Hated the Hero

rows.text: The book's we're all a good story line. There was quite a few mistakes with the wording in all three book's! In the last book about Jacob and Alex, the word's used (in my opinion) for his dick and her pussy, which was prick, pole, dick, cock...etc for his and hers was pussy,  lady parts and whatever else was mentioned (to many to remember) In my opinion, stick with one name/term for the body part's! I mean in the same paragraphs you would use 2 or 3 different name's for the same repeating body part. Also there was a lot of mistakes in meaning his or hers when you was talking about the other person...I mean if it was in the guys POV, you would still say his when it was supposed to be hers ...etc Also I think in the book about Jacob, there was a few places that said Jason. The story line and  the length of all three book's was good tho, I loved that about all 3!
rows.title: Loved the box set

rows.text:
What could have been an excellent movie, was ruined by the profanity of the main characters (Danny Glover and his son) as well as the other characters.
Here is the profanity that is riddled throughout the movie.
At the beginning of the movie, "damn' is heard in the background. Shortly after, Danny Glover said, "Hell, I fought Hitler"
The police officer asks, "Who the hell is this?"
"Scratchin' your ass" "Piss off a cow" "Damn, he was non-violent," Hell no!" "What the hell you doin'?"
"Not no, Hell no!" "This is gonna be a hell of a lot more important. It's more important than any damn bus station..." "I got my ass whipped because of some damn bus station." "You got your ass whipped because you broke rules." "No. I got my ass whipped because you would not stand up for me." "You think I didn't want to kill everyone of them redneck sons of bitches that day? Pasty sons of bitches"
"Don't talk back to me about the law you bitch. Get the hell out" "We're going" "Damn sure are"
"Kick his ass" "What you all do with them bastards?"
As one man struck a man, he repeated three times: "You son of a bitch. You son of a bitch. You son of a bitch."
"I don't want to marry your ugly-ass daughter."
"Hell why don't you just take off." "Oh my God!"
"We got our asses kicked." "Dora got arrested sittin' in the damn bus station. The damn bus stati
rows.title: Be warned

rows.text:
So, here's the thing - I've read the positive reviews, and the negative reviews, and I find it kind of amazing that everyone's taking this so seriously! I mean, yes, the concerns brought up in the negative reviews are valid, and yes, it's totally disturbing that young women are being trained to equate psychotic and/or stalking behavior with "romance", but come on! This content is obviously an elaborate practical joke, isn't it? ISN'T IT? Please say it is!

Be warned - there be ***SPOILERS*** ahead. Oh, and I have a feeling that this is going to go long, so if you're still reading, brace:

First, you have the male protagonist, underground fighter Travis, the Mary Sue-est Mary Sue who ever Mary Sued (well, unless you count the female protagonist, Abby). Is there a male version of Mary Sue? I don't know, but I assure you, that's what he is!

Let's look at the evidence, shall we?

1) He's perfectly built, with a cut six pack and a ton of muscles, without ever doing a single thing to get that way - he doesn't work out, he doesn't train for his fights, he doesn't participate in athletics, he drinks and smokes like it's going out of style, and he eats nothing but junk food. Sure, some people have naturally lean/strong/whatever body types, but a six-pack simply doesn't come out of nowhere. It just doesn't. Not ever.

2
rows.title: I don't know about "Beautiful," but "Disaster" feels about right.

rows.text:
I am not the first person to call Lyndon Johnson a murderer.

And neither was E. Howard Hunt, the longtime CIA operative who organized the Bay of Pigs fiasco and who hated the Kennedys, and who before he died told his son St. John Hunt that Lyndon Johnson and the CIA colluded to murder John Kennedy. (2007)

Nor was Barr McClellan who was a lawyer for Lyndon Johnson and who wrote the book, "Blood, Money & Power: How LBJ Killed JFK" (2003).

Nor was Billie Sol Estes (still alive today 2012 & never interviewed by Caro) who was Lyndon Johnson's cut-out for doing business and kick backs on a massive scale (multiple $ millions). Estes, at the urging of Texas Ranger Clint Peoples, himself a close observer of LBJ's corruption for decades, went to court in the 1980's and confessed to a grand jury that Lyndon Johnson, Cliff Carter, he and Malcolm Wallace planned the murder of US Agricultural official Henry Marshall in June, 1961. The Marshall murder had been classified by Johnson cronies as death by suicide by 5 shots of a bolt action rifle - a now hilarious miscarriage of justice ruling in 1961.

Nor was Madeleine Brown, an inner circle mistress of Lyndon Johnson who had a son Steven Mark in 1950 with LBJ, and for 20 years before her death in 2003 told thousands of people that Lyndon Johnson had foreknowledge of the JFK assassination. LBJ called h
rows.title: Lyndon Johnson's "Passage to Power" was using Military Intelligence to Murder John Kennedy

rows.text:
I can't finish it. I'm sorry, but it is so tacky and cliché. I will read a little, and then decide "nope, can't do it", and then come to return it for a refund only to be reminded that it was free. So then I will read another book, need something else to read, and think "oh, it can't be that bad, I'll trudge through it", and get a few pages before giving up again.

It is such a badly written, clichéd vampire romance. Like to the extreme. Everyone is ridiculously hot and irresistible. Sydney was ridiculously horny--she is always going out like she HAS to have sex or...well, I don't know, it never says. It is almost like the later Anita Blake ardeur (blech). But in this book it is just tacky and superfluous. It doesn’t have great beginning that the AB books had (man, I mourn for those books—they went off the rails so badly, I can’t even skim them just to find out what happens to the characters I liked so much!)

And the story so far is wildly inappropriate. One of the first scenes, Sydney (police detective) has just gotten to a scene with a dead body-a woman tortured, tattooed, and killed. She is standing over the body while the medical examiner (also insanely hot, apparently) talking about the murder, and I think literally in one breath they are talking about how utterly horrific the death was, the next talking about how they want t
rows.title: I can't finish it.

rows.text:
"THE STREET JOURNALIST" FOUND THESE "YOUNG" PEOPLE AND LEFT THESE YOUNG PEOPLE AS HE FOUND THEM. HE PROVIDED NO HELP, JUST CRITICISM. ANY HELP OR INFORMATION TO PERHAPS BETTER THEIR SITUATIONS AS YOUNG MEN AND WOMEN OF THE FUTURE WOULD HAVE BEEN A GREAT CONTRAST. It was very tiring to hear ''monologues'' from Dr. Phil and the Oprah Show about not having a boy in the home: the pop-psychology stuff. I am so sick and tired of hearing those cliches. I'm thinking of the damage it could do on boys who fathers die in the war or who die in car accidents of the thousands of other ways a boy's father or a girl's mother may not be there because they died. Or, because they just up and left, SO!!! So society attacks these children and kill their spirit with this psycho-mumbo jumbo. What I want children to know is there is a thousand ways to have a life and a thousand and one ways to create and live a "style"--and AMERICA loves this grand mix of ways and things. It sparks vitalities, new ideas and ideals and new inventions and concepts. Everybody should have a right to live their story but we cut the legs out from under a percentage of the population with:
"Oh! Raised without your father, a father--Oh! raised without a mother....hmmm; that's too bad.
That's why you will always be poor and ignorant and violent and drug-using and divorced.<
rows.title: A TIMELESS CONTROVERSY ...MARRIAGE IS,  BUT MODERN YOUTH HAVE MUCH "RHECTORIC" TO DEAL WITH,,,,

rows.text: Seriously, this book was bad. I could hardly get through it, and in fact I stopped somewhere along the 40% mark. There was little to no plot, and the pacing was too fast and nothing was explained. The demon father, bitchy mother (whose ass she kisses throughout even though she is treated like crap by her), and her whole relationship with Ethan. One minute she's pissed off at the way he treats her, and doesn't trust him, then two minutes later he's the love of her afterlife, and mates forever with him. Yeah. Sure.  The "heroine" was a bad combination of Mary Sue, and TSTL. She tries to come across as a badass bitch who doesn't care about being pc or different but she's a wimp. Litttle Miss Perfect is all I could think. Gee, you become a vampire, and automatically you're the hottest, strongest, and oh yeah savior of all vampkind..and the hottest prince around wants to mate with you forever. I'm also confused with the whole Gemma sitch.. ok so what is she exactly? Why does she not freak out when she finds out her bff is a vampire.. I mean ok so a part of me would be kinda like cool, but what about that part of you that freaks the f$@& out over there being actual vampires.. Don't even get me started on the whole Prada addiction, we get it you're all Prada whores, do you need to repeat it every two seconds? The whole "story line" seems unrealistic (ok I get this is s
rows.title: Confused with all the 5 star ratings..

rows.text:
I was super excited to finally read Touch of Frost, but sadly, it just did not work for me. I love "special" school settings, so I was eager to learn about Mythos Academy. I had thought it was for the children of the gods, but it's really for all kinds of supposedly myth based races: Spartans, Amazon, Valkyrie, Ninjas, etc. That was pretty awesome. Of course, our narrator, Gwen, is special among the special, as she's a Gypsy. She can touch objects and people and see memories and feel emotions attached to them. It's this ability that helps her figure out what happened when one of her classmates turns up dead in the library and an ancient relic is stolen.

Touch of Frost and I didn't really get off to the best start. The opening chapter is really silly and totally high school drama. It did serve its purpose of introducing us to Gwen's power, since we get to see her in action. And we learn her place in the social heirarchy: she's at the bottom. No surprise there. But it's not the kind of first impression that bodes well for the rest of the book.

There is one issue I had that isn't specific to Touch of Frost, but is something I've noted about the author's other series as well. There is a lot of repetition, and I actually think it's worse than it was in her Elemental Assassin series. Gwen reminds us that she's a Gypsy, and that she has a Gypsy power, and
rows.title: Touch of Frost
